title: hieracium murorum subsp. cophodon
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. cophodon, also known as Hawkweed or Hieracium, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is known for its bitter, milky sap and yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. cophodon are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. cophodon can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
